problem.title: Remove Duplicates from Sorted List II
problem.description: Given the head of a sorted linked list, delete all nodes that have duplicate numbers, leaving only distinct numbers from the original list.
assumptions: Remove ALL occurrences of duplicated values (unlike version I which keeps one).

example.explanation: Remove all nodes that had duplicates (3 and 4 are gone entirely). Use dummy head.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Two Pointers [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Use a dummy head and a prev pointer that always points to the last confirmed unique node. When consecutive equal values are detected, fast-forward past all of them and reconnect prev directly to the node after the duplicated group, skipping every occurrence of that value.
solutions.code_python:
from typing import Optional

# class ListNode:
#     def __init__(self, val=0, next=None):
#         self.val = val
#         self.next = next

def deleteDuplicates(head: Optional[object]) -> Optional[object]:
    dummy = type('ListNode', (), {'val': 0, 'next': head})()
    prev = dummy
    current = head

    while current and current.next:
        if current.val == current.next.val:
            # Skip all nodes with this duplicate value
            dup_val = current.val
            while current and current.val == dup_val:
                current = current.next
            prev.next = current
        else:
            prev = current
            current = current.next

    return dummy.next
